goals.title: Freeze baseline and repo map
goals.objective: Capture the current git, docs, runtime, test, data, and artifact layout before more cleanup.
goals.rationale: Luke has active dirty work, tracked generated proof packs, ignored runtime state, and multiple docs claiming current truth.
scope: Record git status, diff stat, branch, recent commits, tracked generated files, ignored runtime directories, and canonical docs.
Map the high-risk modules: lib/slash-commands.js, trading/router.js, trading execution files, proof scripts, docs, research modules, state/data paths.
goals.stop_condition: A baseline report exists with exact paths and the next cleanup order is confirmed.
acceptance_criteria: The report identifies canonical docs and generated artifact locations.
The report lists high-risk duplicate implementations and live-safety contradictions.
The report includes exact commands used for inspection.
risks: A dirty branch may contain intentional user work that must not be reverted.
assumptions: The current branch is the correct cleanup branch unless the user says otherwise.

goals.title: Consolidate docs and artifacts
goals.objective: Make the documentation source-of-truth chain clear and move generated run output out of top-level docs.
goals.rationale: Duplicate audit/review docs create conflicting truth after every large audit run.
scope: Keep HOSTILE_AUDIT_REPORT.md as canonical audit.
Keep CURRENT_STATUS.md as short snapshot.
Keep focused domain docs for durable curated detail.
Route generated reports to artifacts unless deliberately promoted.
Document the docs map in docs/README.md.
non_goals: Do not erase historical research context without a replacement pointer.
Do not rewrite every research doc in one pass.
goals.stop_condition: Top-level docs no longer contain generated duplicate audit files and canonical pointers agree.
acceptance_criteria: docs/README.md identifies canonical current docs.
Generated Pine slippage detail writes under artifacts/research/pine-slippage-audit.
Current status does not outrun the canonical audit.
validation: cmd /c npx vitest run tests/current-status-docs.test.js tests/pine-slippage-audit.test.js
risks: Some docs are generated by scripts and will reappear unless generators are changed.
assumptions: Historical report details can live in ignored artifacts with a canonical summary in docs.

goals.title: Stabilize runtime and launch paths
goals.objective: Remove recurring shell-window/process-spawn issues and make Luke startup deterministic.
goals.rationale: Proof scripts, desktop helpers, and direct server starts can create port conflicts or transient Windows shells.
scope: Centralize proof app boot and free-port selection.
Use hidden direct child-process calls for desktop helpers.
Review Electron, PM2, root launcher, scripts/windows launchers, and direct node index.js paths.
Resolve or document PM2 hardcoded path assumptions.
non_goals: No installation or service manager migration without user approval.
goals.stop_condition: Luke starts once, PM2 stays online, and proof scripts do not collide with port 3000.
acceptance_criteria: No leftover proof runner processes after verification.
/api/health returns Luke on port 3000 after PM2 reload.
Repeated proof runs use non-conflicting ports when Luke is already running.
validation: cmd /c npx vitest run tests/windows-runtime-spawn.test.js tests/runtime-health.test.js
risks: Direct node index.js still needs a friendly already-running behavior or explicit refusal.
assumptions: PM2 remains the local runtime manager.

goals.title: Freeze live and staged execution
goals.objective: Reconcile read-only docs/UI with legacy live-capable routes and execution modules.
goals.rationale: Luke currently presents as read-only/replay while legacy chat and trading router still expose staged/live execution paths.
scope: Inspect /execute-staged, live, paper, shadow, broker, and chat execution UI paths.
Add or strengthen an explicit hard gate for any route that could submit or stage a trade.
Fix legacy ES/MES point-value risk math or quarantine the legacy path until fixed.
Document any remaining live execution files as blocked and unapproved.
non_goals: Do not remove broker code before its replacement or quarantine plan is approved.
goals.stop_condition: Operator, trading-window, and chat surfaces agree that execution is blocked unless a future explicit proof phase unlocks it.
acceptance_criteria: No default UI path can call live execution.
Any live route returns blocked without explicit approved conditions.
Point-value/risk math has tests or the path is disabled.
validation: cmd /c npx vitest run tests/trading-ui-readonly.test.js tests/autonomous-recommendation-only.test.js tests/trading-common.test.js tests/prop-risk-gate.test.js
risks: A quick delete could hide useful broker-state evidence; prefer hard gates first.
assumptions: Read-only/replay is the desired product mode for now.

goals.title: Refactor command and router monoliths
goals.objective: Reduce split-brain command handling and trading router bloat without changing sacred command behavior.
goals.rationale: slash-commands.js and trading/router.js remain large, fragile, and partially duplicated with extracted modules.
scope: Finish extracting slash command handlers into lib/commands.
Keep slash-commands.js as a route/dispatch layer.
Split trading route registration from trading services in trading/router.js.
Document or remove temporary aliases such as agents/agent-02b-autonomous.js.
non_goals: Do not rewrite command text wholesale.
Do not change sacred slash behavior without tests.
goals.stop_condition: Command and trading route files are smaller, clearer, and covered by focused smoke tests.
acceptance_criteria: /status, /balance, /saty, /ready, and /alert tests pass.
No duplicate live/research command path remains undocumented.
Temporary shims have explicit TODO/removal conditions or are removed.
validation: cmd /c npx vitest run tests/slash-commands.test.js tests/saty-auto-pull.test.js tests/trading-api-replay-mode.test.js
risks: Large command literals have caused prior patch churn; use small line-grounded edits.
assumptions: Existing command outputs are user-facing contracts.

goals.title: Audit dashboard and UX surfaces
goals.objective: Make the dashboard, operator, trading-window, brain dashboard, and chat surfaces consistent, read-only, and usable.
goals.rationale: The UI mostly works, but legacy chat execution affordances and dense dashboard surfaces need a full pass.
scope: Inspect /shell, /operator-v2, /trading-window, /brain-dashboard, /trading, and /luke.
Verify no overlapping text or broken responsive layouts.
Align read-only language and disabled execution affordances.
Use screenshots and DOM checks for desktop and mobile-sized viewports.
non_goals: No decorative redesign that reduces operator density.
goals.stop_condition: The UI communicates current state, blockers, and read-only status without duplicate or contradictory controls.
acceptance_criteria: Operator and trading surfaces do not expose live execution controls.
Dashboard route opens cleanly and embeds the expected surfaces.
Text does not overflow or overlap in core panels.
validation: cmd /c npx vitest run tests/brain-dashboard.test.js tests/operator-v2-ui.test.js tests/operator-v2-trading-window.test.js tests/trading-ui-readonly.test.js
risks: Browser proof scripts currently duplicate harness code and should be stabilized first if they become flaky.
assumptions: A dense operational dashboard is preferable to a marketing-style redesign.

goals.title: Consolidate research and Pine lanes
goals.objective: Make research, Pine, and watchlist outputs organized, explicitly non-live, and reproducible.
goals.rationale: Research versions and generated reports have accumulated as overlapping files and docs.
scope: Clarify latest active fake-breakdown, ladder-reclaim, Saty/Pine, and prop-risk research docs.
Keep generated run outputs in artifacts.
Make shared risk/account math importable from one canonical module where possible.
Rename or archive research-only duplicates with explicit source-map pointers.
non_goals: Do not delete research variants that still have unique evidence until their disposition is recorded.
Do not promote any research lane above WATCHLIST_ONLY without proof.
goals.stop_condition: Research docs and scripts have a clear latest/current path and generated outputs do not become duplicate docs.
acceptance_criteria: TradingView hard-mode research points at artifact reports.
Fake-breakdown and ladder-reclaim docs identify current active versions.
Risk/account rule duplication is reduced or scheduled with exact module ownership.
validation: cmd /c npx vitest run tests/tradingview-level-export.test.js tests/pine-hardmode-slippage.test.js tests/pine-slippage-audit.test.js tests/multi-source-ladder-reclaim-evaluator.test.js
risks: Historical research generated from stale or partial data can look more authoritative than it is.
assumptions: Research output remains watchlist/replay unless a later proof phase says otherwise.

goals.title: Clean state, data, tests, and proof harness
goals.objective: Stop tests and agents from mutating live repo state and reduce proof-script duplication.
goals.rationale: Several tests back up and rewrite real data/state files, while proof scripts duplicate boot/fetch/screenshot logic.
scope: Move agent runtime outputs into lib/paths-managed state locations.
Refactor tests to use temp roots or injected paths instead of live data/state files.
Expand scripts/proof-runtime.js into a shared proof harness.
Decide whether tracked reports/katbot-owner-proof should move to artifacts/proof.
non_goals: Do not delete ignored state/runtime data without a reviewed cleanup list.
Do not break existing proof report consumers.
goals.stop_condition: Tests leave the repo clean, proof scripts share a harness, and generated proof output has an intentional home.
acceptance_criteria: Focused tests do not dirty git status.
Agent state files no longer target repo root.
Proof scripts share boot/fetch helpers or have a documented exception.
validation: cmd /c npx vitest run tests/runtime-health.test.js tests/operator-surface-comparison-script.test.js tests/slash-commands.test.js
git status --short before and after focused tests
risks: Some runtime files are ignored and may be actively used by the local app.
assumptions: Repo cleanliness is a completion requirement, not just cosmetic cleanup.

goals.title: Final proof and missing-piece report
goals.objective: Produce the final handoff showing what is complete, what is still missing besides live data, and what requires manual signoff.
goals.rationale: The user needs a trustworthy handoff after a large cleanup and completion pass.
scope: Run focused and full verification suites.
Reload PM2 and collect health.
Update canonical docs only with verified truth.
Commit coherent cleanup slices.
Write a missing-piece report split into live data, non-live engineering gaps, human signoffs, and optional future work.
non_goals: Do not hide skipped tests or sandbox failures.
goals.stop_condition: The repo has a clean or intentionally documented status and a final reviewed missing-piece list.
acceptance_criteria: Final report includes exact commits, commands, pass/fail results, and residual risks.
Non-live missing work is separated from live-data blockers.
The branch is ready for user review or push.
risks: Full proof suite may require market-hours, network, or manual TradingView steps.
assumptions: Manual signoffs can be listed even when not completed by Codex.
